mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.5% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.39% suggests easing environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk, causing equity investors to reduce exposure and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% and increases by more than 0.3 percentage points within 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.6% is below trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in retail sales below the prior 3-month trend signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as growth forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 1.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.5% year-over-year combined with inflation (CPIAUCSL) remaining below 3.5% signals healthy real supply expansion without stagflation risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth > 1.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 109.5645 and CPIAUCSL at 255.155
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction term has Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1200 thousand units annualized signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as construction and related sectors contract.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1200 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1235.0 thousand units, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive power of HOUST declines to Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 100 thousand new jobs per month combined with rising unemployment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100k AND UNRATE rises by > 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction term Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 3.5% year-over-year and the federal funds rate remains below 3.0%, real rates are negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate continued policy support.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.39% and CPIAUCSL at 255.155
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized combined with declining retail sales signals an approaching demand recession that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are cut.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS declines by > 1.0% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 18907.517 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction term Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
